notes: Covers paid firemen employed by a municipality, village or fire district as regular members. Caused by overexertion in times of stress or danger OR by proximate exposure OR cumulative exposure over 4+ years to heat, smoke, chemical fumes or toxic gases. Applies to paid firefighters only (not volunteers). Covered diseases are not subject to the limitations of § 72-439.

Covers paid firemen employed by municipality, village or fire district as regular members. Caused by overexertion or cumulative exposure over 4+ years to heat, smoke, chemical fumes or toxic gases. Paid firefighters only.

condition_specific: AIDS, AIDS related complexes (ARC), other manifestations of HIV infection, infectious hepatitis viruses, tuberculosis
notes: Applies to occupations involving exposure to human blood or body fluids, which encompasses firefighters. No specific service minimum stated for this provision.

condition_specific: brain cancer (10 yrs), bladder cancer (12 yrs), kidney cancer (15 yrs), colorectal cancer (10 yrs), non-Hodgkin's lymphoma (15 yrs), leukemia (5 yrs), mesothelioma (10 yrs), testicular cancer (5 yrs, age <40), breast cancer (5 yrs, age <40), esophageal cancer (10 yrs), multiple myeloma (15 yrs)
filing_deadline_notes: Presumption does not apply to any disease diagnosed more than 10 years following the last date on which the firefighter actually worked as a firefighter
notes: Each cancer type has its own minimum years-of-service threshold (5-15 years). 'Firefighter' = employee whose primary duty is extinguishing or investigating fires. Pre-employment medical screening exam must not have revealed the disease. Presumption is rebuttable by substantial evidence. Post-termination window is 10 years (120 months) from last date worked. Tobacco exclusion: presumption does not apply if firefighter or firefighter's cohabitant has regularly and habitually used tobacco products for 10+ years prior to diagnosis. Testicular cancer: age <40 and no evidence of anabolic steroids or human growth hormone use. Breast cancer: age <40 without breast cancer genetic predisposition.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury (PTSD or PTSI per DSM-5)
notes: Requires diagnosis by a psychologist, licensed psychiatrist, or counselor trained in PTSI; and clear and convincing evidence the injury was caused by an event or events arising out of employment. Originally had a sunset of July 1, 2023 (Section 2 of the act), but effective for dates of injury/manifestation on or after July 1, 2019. Applies to firefighters as defined in §§ 59-1391(f) and 72-1403(A).

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury (PTSD or PTSI per DSM-5)
notes: Applies to peace officers (city, county, Idaho state police) as defined in § 19-5101(d). Requires PTSI diagnosis and clear and convincing evidence. Originally had a sunset of July 1, 2023. Effective for dates of injury/manifestation on or after July 1, 2019.

condition_specific: post-traumatic stress injury (PTSD or PTSI per DSM-5)
notes: Applies to EMS providers certified under §§ 56-1011 through 56-1018B and ambulance-based clinicians. Requires PTSI diagnosis and clear and convincing evidence. Originally had a sunset of July 1, 2023. Effective for dates of injury/manifestation on or after July 1, 2019.
